lede: A look at November 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Christian Convery and Marcus Browne, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Armenia
events.desc: Armenia and Azerbaijan sign a ceasefire agreement, ending the Second Nagorno-Karabakh War, and prompting protests in Armenia.

events.title: Daecheong incident
events.desc: Ships of the South and North Korean navies skirmish off Daecheong Island in the Yellow Sea.

events.title: SS Edmund Fitzgerald
events.desc: The 729-foot-long freighter SS Edmund Fitzgerald sinks during a storm on Lake Superior, killing all 29 crew on board.

events.title: Israeli–Palestinian conflict
events.desc: Israeli-Palestinian conflict: the United Nations General Assembly passes Resolution 3379, determining that Zionism is a form of racism.

events.title: 1940 Vrancea earthquake
events.desc: The 1940 Vrancea earthquake strikes Romania killing an estimated 1,000 and injuring approximately 4,000 more.

events.title: Frans Eemil Sillanpää
events.desc: Finnish author F. E. Sillanpää is awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature.

events.title: White supremacy
events.desc: White supremacists seized power and massacred black Americans during the Wilmington massacre, the only instance of a municipal government being overthrown in United States history.

events.title: Henry Morton Stanley
events.desc: Henry Morton Stanley locates missing explorer and missionary, David Livingstone in Ujiji, near Lake Tanganyika, famously greeting him with the words, "Dr. Livingstone, I presume?"

events.title: Henry Wirz
events.desc: Major Henry Wirz, the superintendent of a prison camp in Andersonville, Georgia, is hanged, becoming one of only three American Civil War soldiers executed for war crimes.

events.title: Fourth Crusade
events.desc: Fourth Crusade: Despite letters from Pope Innocent III forbidding it and threatening excommunication, Catholic crusaders begin a siege of Zara (now Zadar, Croatia).

births.name: Ernst Otto Fischer
births.line: German chemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate (died 2007)

births.name: Heinrich XXVII, Prince Reuss Younger Line

deaths.text: Nadarajah Raviraj, Sri Lankan lawyer and politician
Dick the Bruiser, American football player and wrestler
Henry Wirz, Swiss-American captain in Confederate army, commandant of Andersonville Prison
